content_en:
“My name is Bahman Jathwiyah, and the whole world knows me and stands in awe of me. Look at my pomp and authority and experience at first hand my magnificence and grandeur. I have come today as your nemesis and a calamity. Who can face my sharp sword? You will face me? Ha Ha Ha! Go young lad

and send someone else. You will not be able to withstand even one of the stabs I will make. This war is for men, and not for youths. Go, lad, and enjoy the pleasures of life to the full and some experienced person forward to fight against me.”

When the sun set the next evening and the darkness of night had spread over four corner of the earth, the fighting stopped. The armies went to their encampments to rest. The Mujahideen went to sleep to regain their energies for the following day. But the one person who could not rest was Qa’qa’ 4%, who was using his quick and fertile mind to plan a comprehensive strategy for the third day. Sa‘d bin Abu

“O believers of the Allah, if you wish to bathe the bodies of the martyrs do so, or if you wish to bury them covered with their bleeding wounds you are free to do so. Because the Prophet 38 has said, ‘the martyr will be raised on the Day of Judgement with his bleeding wounds smelling like saffron.”

The martyrs were buried with full military honours and the wounds of the injured were treated.

When the sun rose on the morning of the third day, both the armies came out on the battlefield. Again the mammoth elephants were seen with the Iranian army in the battlefield. Sa‘d bin Abu Wags -% sent for those Iranian soldiers who had accepted Islam, in order to consult with them. He asked them what was the most effective way to make these giant elephants retreat. They told him to aimat the eyes and trunks of the elephants; the result would be that they would race backwards out of the battlefield, screeching and screaming and destroying their own army. Sa’d bin Abu Waqas -# then sent for Qa’ qa’ and his brother, ‘Asim bin ‘Amr - and revealing this secret, told them to try it out that day. So the two brothers targeted the white elephant’s eyes. Simultaneously, Qa’qa’ bin ‘Amr displaying great courage and valour attacked the trunk of the elephant with his sword; it fell far away on the ground as he cut it off. The elephant, in a mad rage, ran screaming; its rider fell on the ground and many Iranian soldiers were trampled under it. Similarly another Mujahid attacked another elephant and targeted its eyes and trunk. The animal was blinded and started to scamper around the battlefield causing confusion. The fierce battle continued till darkness began to spread; the whole atmosphere was polluted with dirt and dust, and as a consequence one could not see clearly who was attacking whom, and whose sword was slitting whose throat. Yet the battle continued the whole night until it was almost dawn. Qa’qa’ bin ‘Amr At-Tameemi + was going around the battlefield and mowing down anyone who came in front of him. Sa’d bin Abu Wags - was sitting on the foremost part of the ramparts and offering supplications to Allah for him.

Fighting his way through the ranks of the enemy, Hilal bin Alqama finally succeeded in reaching the place where the Iranian General, Rustum had entrenched himself. When he saw that his safety was endangered, he fled. In front of him was a stream; in order to save his life he jumped in. Hilal bin Alqama also jumped in, in pursuit of him. They both started wrestling in the middle of the stream, but Rustum was no match for Hilal bin Alqama. At last the latter inflicted a fatal wound on Rustum, and he collapsed in the water. Catching him by his

feet Hilal bin Alqama, dragged him out of the water, and chopped off his head with his sword. Then he announced loudly:

“By the Lord of the Ka’bah I have slain the Iranian General, Rustum, Look! The body of the enemy of Allah has become a sign of warning and an admonition for all men.”

The Iranian army was demoralised on seeing their General’s dead body, and was forced to surrender in the battlefield of Qadissiyah. This day is remembered in history as Youm-e-'Ammas.

“The most vital role at Qadissiyah was played by Qa’qa’ bin ‘Amr At-Tameemi 4. I was astounded to see him so passionately moved by the spirit of Jihad that using his physical strength he assaulted the enemy thirty times. In every attack he killed an Iranian General. There is no doubt that his exploits in Qadissiyah deserve a golden chapter in the military history of Islam.”

The great General Sa’d bin Abu Waqas #% decided to attack Madyan, but there was a river which needed to be crossed. The Iranians had destroyed all the bridges to prevent the Muslim army from entering the city by crossing them. After a great deal of consideration it was decided to set off on the journey invoking the Name of Allah and cross the stream on horseback. Meanwhile the Iranian forces were waiting on the opposite riverbank.

“These are Monsters, Giants and Jinn who are coming to swallow us!”

The Muslim army reached the opposite bank safely and entered the city of Madyan under their Commander, Sa’d bin Abu Waqas #. The people of the city had also run away to save their lives, leaving their homes empty; the city was completely deserted. The scene was certainly a warning for the discerning mind and sensitive soul. Madyan was taken peacefully, without any resistance. The Muslim army continued on its onward march towards its objective. The famous Arab heroes continued their mission and fighting, sometimes as generals and commanders and at times even as ordinary soldiers. In spite of winning numerous victories they did not amass wealth nor did they hunger for power and sovereignty over conquered lands, only one image dominated their minds - ‘The pre-eminence of Islam and honourable crown of martyrdom.’

Jalula’ is the name of an important centrally located city on the road to Khurasan. It is about forty miles from Madyan. Many roads lead from here to various other towns in Iran. A long river flows by the side of the city and many large and small ships ply on it. The people of Madyan had fled from their city and found refuge here. They wanted to make this city a centre from which they could organise a movement to resist the Arabs; they felt that if they did not unite against the Arabs, the Iranian people would be destroyed forever. There would be no safe-haven for them anywhere in the world, Their leader Yazdagar had fled from Madyan and taken refuge. From here he sent a huge Iranian army under the Iranian General, Mehran Razi to Jalula’ to give a befitting reply to the Arab Mujahideen. Iranian army dug a huge trench around the city, so that it could provide a defence for the city.

Qa’qa’ bin ‘Amr besieged the city of Jalula’ and the Iranians shut themselves up in the fort. In order to arouse the passion for Jihad among the soldiers, the Commander-in-Chief, Hisham bin Utbah 4 gave an emotionally charged speech. He said:

“For the sake of Allah! Conduct your life in these times of trial in a disciplined manner. He will grant you a reward in the Hereafter and also you will get a lot of booty here. And all you do, should be to gain the reward from Allah.”

After listening to the stirring speech of his leader, Qa‘qa’ bin ‘Amr > launched a vigorous attack. Coming down to the entrance of the trenches, he shouted:

“Muslims your Commander-in-Chief has got down into the trenches; follow him quickly. Now we have to cross it and enter the city - this is vital. There is no other way to succeed; see that no person stays back.”
content_id:
"Namaku Bahman Jathwiyah, dan seluruh dunia mengenalku serta gentar terhadapku. Lihatlah kemegahan dan kekuasaanku, dan saksikan sendiri keagungan dan kebesaranku. Hari ini aku datang sebagai malaperakamu dan bencana bagimu. Siapa yang sanggup menghadapi pedangku yang tajam? Kau akan menghadapiku? Ha Ha Ha! Pergilah anak muda

dan kirimkan orang lain. Kau tidak akan mampu menahan bahkan satu tusukan dariku. Perang ini untuk para lelaki, bukan untuk para pemuda. Pergilah, anak muda, nikmatilah kesenangan hidup sepuasnya dan kirimkan orang yang berpengalaman untuk bertempur melawanku."

Ketika matahari terbenam pada sore berikutnya dan kegelapan malam telah menyelimuti empat penjuru bumi, pertempuran berhenti. Kedua pasukan kembali ke perkemahan mereka untuk beristirahat. Para mujahidin tertidur untuk memulihkan tenaga mereka untuk hari berikutnya. Namun satu orang yang tidak dapat beristirahat adalah Qa'qa' yang menggunakan pikirannya yang cepat dan cerdas untuk merancang strategi menyeluruh untuk hari ketiga. Sa'd bin Abu

"Wahai orang-orang yang beriman kepada Allah, jika kalian ingin memandikan jenazah para syuhada, lakukanlah, atau jika kalian ingin menguburkan mereka dengan luka-luka berdarah mereka, kalian bebas melakukannya. Karena Nabi ﷺ telah bersabda, 'Orang yang mati syahid akan dibangkitkan pada Hari Kiamat dengan luka-lukanya yang berdarah berbau seperti za'faran.'"

Para syuhada dimakamkan dengan penghormatan militer penuh dan luka-luka para prajurit yang terluka diobati.

Ketika matahari terbit pada pagi hari ketiga, kedua pasukan keluar ke medan perang. Sekali lagi gajah-gajah raksasa terlihat bersama pasukan Iran di medan tempur. Sa'd bin Abu Waqqas mengirim utusan untuk memanggil para prajurit Iran yang telah memeluk Islam, guna bermusyawarah dengan mereka. Beliau bertanya kepada mereka tentang cara paling efektif untuk membuat gajah-gajah raksasa itu mundur. Mereka menyarankan untuk membidik mata dan belalai gajah; hasilnya gajah-gajah itu akan berlari mundur keluar medan perang, meraung dan menjerit serta menghancurkan pasukan mereka sendiri. Sa'd bin Abu Waqqas kemudian memanggil Qa'qa' dan saudaranya, 'Asim bin 'Amr, dan mengungkapkan rahasia ini, memerintahkan mereka untuk mencobanya hari itu. Maka kedua bersaudara itu membidik mata gajah putih. Secara bersamaan, Qa'qa' bin 'Amr menunjukkan keberanian dan kepahlawanan luar biasa menyerang belalai gajah itu dengan pedangnya; belalai itu jatuh jauh di tanah setelah dipotongnya. Gajah itu, dalam kemurkaan yang luar biasa, berlari sambil meraung; pengendaranya jatuh ke tanah dan banyak prajurit Iran terinjak di bawahnya. Demikian pula seorang mujahid lainnya menyerang gajah lain dan membidik mata serta belalainya. Hewan itu menjadi buta dan mulai berlarian di medan perang menyebabkan kekacauan. Pertempuran sengit berlanjut hingga kegelapan mulai menyebar; seluruh suasana dipenuhi debu dan kotoran, sehingga akibatnya seseorang tidak dapat melihat dengan jelas siapa yang menyerang siapa, dan pedang siapa yang membelah leher siapa. Namun pertempuran terus berlanjut sepanjang malam hingga hampir fajar. Qa'qa' bin 'Amr At-Tameemi berkeliling medan perang dan menebaskan pedangnya kepada siapa pun yang muncul di hadapannya. Sa'd bin Abu Waqqas duduk di bagian terdepan benteng dan memanjatkan du'a kepada Allah untuknya.

Bertempur menembus barisan musuh, Hilal bin Alqama akhirnya berhasil mencapai tempat di mana Jenderal Iran, Rustum, telah membentengi dirinya. Ketika Rustum melihat bahwa keselamatannya terancam, ia melarikan diri. Di hadapannya ada sebuah sungai; untuk menyelamatkan nyawanya ia melompat ke dalamnya. Hilal bin Alqama juga melompat ke dalam sungai, mengejarnya. Keduanya mulai bergulat di tengah sungai, namun Rustum bukan tandingan Hilal bin Alqama. Akhirnya Hilal bin Alqama menghujamkan luka fatal pada Rustum, dan ia roboh di dalam air. Menangkap

kakinya, Hilal bin Alqama menyeretnya keluar dari air, dan memenggal kepalanya dengan pedangnya. Kemudian ia mengumumkan dengan lantang:

"Demi Tuhan pemilik Ka'bah, aku telah membunuh Jenderal Iran, Rustum. Lihatlah! Jasad musuh Allah ini telah menjadi tanda peringatan dan pelajaran bagi semua manusia."

Pasukan Iran menjadi demoralisasi melihat jasad Jenderal mereka, dan terpaksa menyerah di medan perang Qadisiyyah. Hari ini dikenang dalam sejarah sebagai Yaum al-'Ammas.

"Peran paling vital di Qadisiyyah dimainkan oleh Qa'qa' bin 'Amr At-Tameemi. Aku takjub melihatnya begitu bergairah digerakkan oleh semangat jihad sehingga dengan menggunakan kekuatan fisiknya ia menyerbu musuh tiga puluh kali. Dalam setiap serangan ia membunuh seorang Jenderal Iran. Tidak diragukan lagi bahwa eksploitasinya di Qadisiyyah layak mendapat bab emas dalam sejarah militer Islam."

Jenderal besar Sa'd bin Abu Waqqas memutuskan untuk menyerang Madyan, tetapi ada sebuah sungai yang harus diseberangi. Pasukan Iran telah menghancurkan semua jembatan untuk mencegah pasukan Muslim memasuki kota dengan menyeberanginya. Setelah pertimbangan yang panjang, diputuskan untuk berangkat dalam perjalanan dengan menyebut Nama Allah dan menyeberangi sungai dengan menunggang kuda. Sementara itu pasukan Iran menunggu di tepi sungai seberang.

"Mereka adalah monster, raksasa, dan jin yang datang untuk menelan kita!"

Pasukan Muslim tiba di tepi seberang dengan selamat dan memasuki kota Madyan di bawah pimpinan Komandan mereka, Sa'd bin Abu Waqqas. Penduduk kota juga telah melarikan diri untuk menyelamatkan nyawa mereka, meninggalkan rumah-rumah mereka kosong; kota itu benar-benar sepi. Pemandangan itu sungguh menjadi peringatan bagi pikiran yang cerdas dan jiwa yang peka. Madyan diambil secara damai, tanpa perlawanan apa pun. Pasukan Muslim melanjutkan perjalanan maju mereka menuju tujuan. Para pahlawan Arab yang terkenal melanjutkan misi dan pertempuran mereka, terkadang sebagai jenderal dan komandan, dan di lain waktu bahkan sebagai prajurit biasa. Meskipun memenangkan banyak kemenangan, mereka tidak menumpuk kekayaan dan tidak pula menginginkan kekuasaan serta kedaulatan atas tanah-tanah yang ditaklukkan, hanya satu citra yang mendominasi pikiran mereka - 'Keunggulan Islam dan mahkota terhormat kesyahidan.'

Jalula' adalah nama sebuah kota penting yang terletak di pusat, di jalan menuju Khurasan. Kota ini berjarak sekitar empat puluh mil dari Madyan. Banyak jalan menuju dari sini ke berbagai kota lain di Iran. Sebuah sungai panjang mengalir di sisi kota dan banyak kapal besar dan kecil berlayar di atasnya. Penduduk Madyan yang telah melarikan diri dari kota mereka menemukan perlindungan di sini. Mereka ingin menjadikan kota ini sebagai pusat dari mana mereka bisa mengorganisir gerakan perlawanan terhadap bangsa Arab; mereka merasa bahwa jika mereka tidak bersatu melawan bangsa Arab, bangsa Iran akan hancur selamanya. Tidak akan ada tempat berlindung yang aman bagi mereka di mana pun di dunia. Pemimpin mereka Yazdagar telah melarikan diri dari Madyan dan berlindung di sini. Dari sini ia mengirim pasukan Iran yang besar di bawah Jenderal Iran, Mehran Razi ke Jalula' untuk memberikan balasan yang setimpal kepada para mujahidin Arab. Pasukan Iran menggali parit besar di sekeliling kota, agar dapat memberikan pertahanan bagi kota tersebut.

Qa'qa' bin 'Amr mengepung kota Jalula' dan pasukan Iran mengurung diri mereka di dalam benteng. Untuk membangkitkan semangat jihad di kalangan prajurit, Panglima Tertinggi, Hisham bin Utbah memberikan pidato yang penuh semangat. Beliau berkata:

"Demi Allah! Jalani kehidupan kalian di masa-masa ujian ini dengan disiplin. Allah akan memberikan kalian pahala di akhirat dan kalian juga akan mendapatkan banyak ghanimah di sini. Dan semua yang kalian lakukan, hendaklah untuk meraih pahala dari Allah."

Setelah mendengarkan pidato yang menggugah dari pemimpinnya, Qa'qa' bin 'Amr melancarkan serangan gencar. Turun ke pintu masuk parit, ia berteriak:

"Kaum Muslimin, Panglima Tertinggi kalian telah turun ke dalam parit; ikutilah beliau dengan cepat. Sekarang kita harus menyeberanginya dan memasuki kota - ini sangat penting. Tidak ada cara lain untuk berhasil; pastikan tidak seorang pun tertinggal."
content_ar: “My name is Bahman Jathwiyah, and the whole الدنيا knows me and stands in awe of me. Look at my pomp and authority and experience at أولاً hand my magnificence and grandeur. I have come today as your nemesis and a calamity. Who can face my sharp السيف? You will face me? Ha Ha Ha! Go young lad and send someone else. You will not be able to withstand even one of the stabs I will make. This war is for men, and not for youths. Go, lad, and enjoy the pleasures of الحياة to the full and some experienced person forward to fight against me.” عندما the sun set the next evening and the darkness of night had spread over four corner of the earth, the fighting stopped. The armies went to their encampments to rest. The المجاهدين went to sleep to regain their energies for the following day. But the one person who could not rest was Qa’qa’ 4%, who was using his quick and fertile العقل to plan a comprehensive strategy for the ثالثاً day. Sa‘d bin Abu “O المؤمنون of the الله, إذا you wish to bathe the bodies of the الشهيدs do لذا, or إذا you wish to bury them covered with their bleeding wounds you are free to do لذا. لأن النبي ﷺ 38 has said, ‘the الشهيد will be raised on the يوم القيامة with his bleeding wounds smelling like saffron.” The الشهيدs were buried with full military honours and the wounds of the injured were treated. عندما the sun rose on the morning of the ثالثاً day, both the armies came out on the المعركةfield. Again the mammoth elephants were seen with the Iranian الجيش in the المعركةfield. Sa‘d bin Abu Wags -% sent for those Iranian soldiers who had accepted الإسلام, من أجل consult with them. He asked them what was the most effective الطريق to make these giant elephants retreat. They told him to aimat the eyes and trunks of the elephants; the result would be that they would race backwards out of the المعركةfield, screeching and screaming and destroying their own الجيش. Sa’d bin Abu Waqas -# then sent for Qa’ qa’ and his الأخ, ‘Asim bin ‘Amr - and revealing this secret, told them to try it out that day. لذا the two brothers targeted the white elephant’s eyes. Simultaneously, Qa’qa’ bin ‘Amr displaying great الشجاعة and valour attacked the trunk of the elephant with his السيف; it fell far away on the ground as he cut it off. The elephant, in a mad rage, ran screaming; its rider fell on the ground and many Iranian soldiers were trampled under it. Similarly another Mujahid attacked another elephant and targeted its eyes and trunk. The animal was blinded and started to scamper around the المعركةfield causing confusion. The fierce المعركة continued till darkness began to spread; the whole atmosphere was polluted with dirt and dust, and as a consequence one could not see clearly who was attacking whom, and whose السيف was slitting whose throat. Yet the المعركة continued the whole night حتى it was almost dawn. Qa’qa’ bin ‘Amr At-Tameemi + was going around the المعركةfield and mowing down anyone who came in front of him. Sa’d bin Abu Wags - was sitting on the foremost part of the ramparts and offering supplications to الله for him. Fighting his الطريق through the ranks of the العدو, Hilal bin Alqama وأخيراً succeeded in reaching the place where the Iranian General, Rustum had entrenched himself. عندما he saw that his safety was endangered, he fled. In front of him was a stream; من أجل save his الحياة he jumped in. Hilal bin Alqama أيضاً jumped in, in pursuit of him. They both started wrestling in the middle of the stream, but Rustum was no match for Hilal bin Alqama. At last the latter inflicted a fatal wound on Rustum, and he collapsed in the water. Catching him by his feet Hilal bin Alqama, dragged him out of the water, and chopped off his head with his السيف. Then he announced loudly: “By the Lord of the Ka’bah I have slain the Iranian General, Rustum, Look! The الجسد of the العدو of الله has become a sign of warning and an admonition for all men.” The Iranian الجيش was demoralised on seeing their General’s dead الجسد, and wأماced to surrender in the المعركةfield of Qadissiyah. This day is remembered in history as Youm-e-'Ammas. “The most الجوهري role at Qadissiyah was played by Qa’qa’ bin ‘Amr At-Tameemi 4. I was astounded to see him لذا passionately moved by the الروح of الجهاد that using his physical القوة he assaulted the العدو thirty times. In every attack he killed an Iranian General. لا شك that his exploits in Qadissiyah deserve a golden الباب in the military history of الإسلام.” The great General Sa’d bin Abu Waqas #% decided to attack Madyan, but there was a river which needed to be crossed. The Iranians had destroyed all the bridges to prevent the مسلم الجيش from entering the city by crossing them. بعد a great deal of consideration it was decided to set off on the journey invoking the Name of الله and cross the stream on horseback. Meanwhile the Iranian forces were waiting on the opposite riverbank. “These are Monsters, Giants and الجن who are coming to swallow us!” The مسلم الجيش reached the opposite bank safely and entered the city of Madyan under their القائد, Sa’d bin Abu Waqas #. The people of the city had أيضاً run away to save their lives, leaving their homes empty; the city was completely deserted. The scene was certainly a warning for the discerning العقل and sensitive النفس. Madyan was taken peacefully, without any resistance. The مسلم الجيش continued on its onward march towards its objective. The famous Arab heroes continued their mission and fighting, sometimes as generals and القائدs and at times even as ordinary soldiers. In spite of winning numerous victories they did not amass الثروة nor did they hunger for power and sovereignty over conquered lands, only one image dominated their minds - ‘The pre-eminence of الإسلام and honourable crown of الشهيدdom.’ Jalula’ is the name of an المهم centrally located city on the road to Khurasan. It is about forty miles from Madyan. Many roads lead from here to various other towns in Iran. A long river flows by the side of the city and many large and small ships ply on it. The people of Madyan had fled from their city and found refuge here. They wanted to make this city a centre from which they could organise a movement to resist the Arabs; they felt that إذا they did not unite against the Arabs, the Iranian people would be destroyed forever. There would be no safe-haven for them anywhere in the الدنيا, Their leader Yazdagar had fled from Madyan and taken refuge. From here he sent a huge Iranian الجيش under the Iranian General, Mehran Razi to Jalula’ to give a befitting reply to the Arab المجاهدين. Iranian الجيش dug a huge trench around the city, لذا that it could provide a defence for the city. Qa’qa’ bin ‘Amr besieged the city of Jalula’ and the Iranians shut themselves up in the fort. من أجل arouse the passion for الجهاد among the soldiers, the القائد-in-Chief, Hisham bin Utbah 4 gave an emotionally charged speech. قال: “For the sake of الله! السلوك your الحياة in these times of الابتلاء in a disciplined manner. He will grant you a الأجر in الآخرة and أيضاً you will get a lot of booty here. And all you do, should be to gain the الأجر from الله.” بعد listening to the stirring speech of his leader, Qa‘qa’ bin ‘Amr > launched a vigorous attack. Coming down to the entrance of the trenches, he shouted: “المسلمون your القائد-in-Chief has got down into the trenches; follow him quickly. Now we have to cross it and enter the city - this is الجوهري. There is no other الطريق to succeed; see that no person stays back.”
